resposta:
solution: 
```class Solution:
    def greatestLetter(self, s: str) -> str:
        upper = set(c for c in s if c.isupper())
        lower = set(c for c in s if c.islower())
        common = upper & lower
        return max(common, default='', key=lambda x: ord(x))
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses set data structures to efficiently find the common letters between uppercase and lowercase letters in the string. It then uses the built-in max function with a custom key function to find the greatest common letter. The key function uses the ord function to compare the ASCII values of the characters, which corresponds to their alphabetical order.
